plan_overview: Full review of a Customer Connections Application — identifies all relevant documents, extracts design brief and site plan information in parallel, then consolidates results into a unified report.

plan_steps.description: Scan the project document folder for all relevant documents. Use the search context and document categorisation rules from the process knowledge files (proc-extract-design-brief-info, proc-extract-site-plan-info, and proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet) to determine which files to include and how to classify each document type. For each file found, read the document content and classify it as one of: 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', 'Town Planning Report', 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', 'Network Diagram', or 'TAL'. Any Excel spreadsheet file (.xlsx or .xls) containing asset register, equipment schedule, or inventory data (columns for Asset ID, Asset Type, Location, Condition Rating, or similar) must be classified as 'TAL' (Technical Asset List). Classification must be based on document content — what the document actually contains — not its filename. Produce a structured document list with metadata for all matched files.
plan_steps.name: Identify and Review All Application Documents

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'documents' key containing a list of all matched files, each with 'document_title', 'document_type', 'confidence', and 'filename'. The 'document_type' field must reflect what the document actually contains based on a content review, not the filename. Spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls) containing asset register or equipment schedule data must have document_type set to TAL.

plan_steps.description: CRITICAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENT: Every entry in the 'processing_plan' array MUST include a 'document_category' field set to exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. This field is mandatory — omitting it will cause step failure. Read the Step 1 document review output file and the tool registry. For each identified document produce a processing plan entry with ALL of the following fields: 'filename', 'document_type', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'recommended_reader', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'conversion_type', 'extraction_method', 'processing_notes', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. For spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) with document_category 'TAL', the processing plan entry will also contain 'worksheet_count' (total sheets in the file), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (non-empty sheets), and 'worksheets' (array of {sheet_name, row_count, column_count}) — these are pre-populated by the agent and must be preserved exactly as provided in the output. To assign 'document_category', apply these rules IN ORDER: RULE 0 - TAL SPREADSHEET: if the file extension is .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb, set document_category to 'TAL' regardless of any other rules. RULE 1 — SITE PLAN FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'RETIC', 'retic', 'DWG', 'dwg', 'SITEPLAN', 'DRAWING', 'SUBSTATION', set document_category to 'Site Plan'. RULE 2 — DESIGN BRIEF FILENAME: if the filename contains any of 'BRIEF', 'brief', 'ARP', 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'ARCHITECTURAL', 'SERVICES', 'TOWN_PLANNING', set document_category to 'Design Brief'. RULE 3 — CONTENT FALLBACK (only when filename has no clear signal): use document_type from Step 1 — 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', or 'Network Diagram' maps to 'Site Plan'; 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', or 'Town Planning Report' maps to 'Design Brief'; 'TAL' maps to 'TAL'. PAGE SIZE AND CHUNKING ASSESSMENT: Open each document and inspect the actual page dimensions. Classify page_size as one of: 'A4' (210Ã—297mm), 'A3' (297Ã—420mm), 'A2' (420Ã—594mm), 'A1' (594Ã—841mm), 'A0' (841Ã—1189mm), or 'large-format' (anything larger). Set requires_chunking to true if page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format' — these pages are too large for a single LLM context pass and must be split. For requires_chunking true, set chunk_strategy to 'quadrant-split' for A2/A1/A0/large-format drawings (split page into overlapping quadrants), or 'page-split' for multi-page text documents where individual pages exceed A3. Set estimated_chunks to the expected number of chunks per page (A2â†’4, A1â†’6, A0â†’9, large-formatâ†’12; use 1 for A4/A3). Set chunk_strategy to 'none' and estimated_chunks to 1 when requires_chunking is false. Example output entry: {"filename": "D526458_ARP4983_RETIC.pdf", "document_type": "Reticulation Drawing", "content_type": "image", "text_quality": "none", "recommended_reader": "ocr", "processing_tool_id": "tool-extract-pdf-content", "conversion_required": true, "conversion_type": "pdf-to-png", "extraction_method": "ocr", "processing_notes": "A1 large-format drawing, requires OCR with quadrant chunking", "document_category": "Site Plan", "page_size": "A1", "requires_chunking": true, "chunk_strategy": "quadrant-split", "estimated_chunks": 6} ROUTING RULES (set route_to_step in every processing plan entry): TAL files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) set route_to_step=6, requires_chunking=false, chunk_strategy="none" — they bypass Steps 3, 4, and 5 and go directly to Step 6 (asset spreadsheet extraction). Design Brief files set route_to_step=4. Site Plan files set route_to_step=5. Add "route_to_step" as a mandatory field in every processing plan entry.
plan_steps.name: Plan document processing approach

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with a 'processing_plan' key (array) and 'total_documents' integer. MANDATORY: every entry in 'processing_plan' must contain ALL of these fields: 'filename', 'processing_tool_id', 'conversion_required', 'content_type', 'text_quality', 'extraction_method', 'document_category', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', and 'estimated_chunks'. The 'document_category' field must be exactly 'Design Brief', 'Site Plan', or 'TAL'. Files with RETIC/DWG/SITEPLAN/DRAWING/SUBSTATION in their filename must have document_category 'Site Plan'. Files with BRIEF/ARP/DESIGN_BRIEF/ARCHITECTURAL/SERVICES/TOWN_PLANNING in their filename must have document_category 'Design Brief'. The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. The 'requires_chunking' field must be true for any entry where page_size is 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When requires_chunking is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' (not 'none') and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1. Files with extension .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb must have document_category 'TAL'. TAL entries must also include 'worksheet_count' (integer), 'worksheets_to_analyse' (integer), and 'worksheets' (array). A missing or null 'document_category' is a critical failure. Every entry must also include 'route_to_step' (integer: 4 for Design Brief, 5 for Site Plan, 6 for TAL). TAL entries must have requires_chunking=false and chunk_strategy="none".

plan_steps.description: Read the Step 2 processing plan and identify all documents where requires_chunking is true AND route_to_step is NOT 6. TAL spreadsheet files (route_to_step=6) do not require chunking and must be excluded from this step — they are processed directly in Step 6. For each such document, call the document chunker with the document's chunk_strategy and page_size to render it into a sequence of PNG image chunks. A quadrant-split A1 drawing produces 6 PNG chunks (3 cols Ã— 2 rows); an A0 drawing produces 9 chunks (3Ã—3); a large-format drawing produces 12 chunks (4Ã—3). Each chunk is a distinct region of the page at 200 DPI. Output a chunk manifest per document listing all generated chunk files in sequence order with their region labels, dimensions, and file paths. Documents where requires_chunking is false are passed through unchanged and listed in documents_not_chunked.
plan_steps.name: Chunk Documents for Processing

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_documents_chunked' (integer â‰¥ 0), 'documents_not_chunked' (list of filenames), and 'chunked_documents' (object keyed by filename). Each entry in 'chunked_documents' must include 'job_id', 'chunk_strategy', 'page_size', 'total_chunks', 'total_pages', and a 'chunks' array. Each chunk entry must have 'chunk_id', 'sequence', 'page_number', 'region', 'filename', 'filepath', 'width_px', and 'height_px'. Documents with requires_chunking=false must not appear in 'chunked_documents'. IMPORTANT: estimated_chunks_per_page in the input is the expected chunk count PER PAGE — the correct total_chunks equals estimated_chunks_per_page multiplied by total_pages; do not flag a mismatch if total_chunks equals estimated_chunks_per_page x total_pages. DPI is an internal rendering setting and must NOT be validated against any expected value.

plan_steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=4 (document_category="Design Brief"). TAL spreadsheet files and Site Plan files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those documents that meet the search context of the 'Extract Information from Design Brief' process (proc-extract-design-brief-info). A document qualifies if its content matches the search context criteria: it must contain a 'Method of Supply Requirement' section and/or a 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section, or be an architectural, services, or town planning report. Do not process any document that does not meet this content criteria, even if it was tagged as 'Design Brief' in Step 2 — and do not process any Site Plan or drawing document. For each qualifying document, extract structured information using proc-extract-design-brief-info as the extraction guide. Extract supply requirements (HV, LV, Street Lighting, Earthing, Easements, Substation) and funding determination items.
plan_steps.name: Extract Design Brief Information

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only documents whose content matches the proc-extract-design-brief-info search context (i.e. documents containing a Method of Supply or Determination of Funding Requirements section). Must not include Site Plan, drawing, or reticulation documents. Each extraction must include 'filename', 'total_sections', 'relevant_sections', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', 'estimated_chunks', and 'step_extraction' with at least one of: hv, lv, sl, earthing, easements, substation, funding_items. The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When 'requires_chunking' is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1.

plan_steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan"). TAL spreadsheet files and Design Brief files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those where 'document_category' is 'Site Plan'. Do not process any document tagged as 'Design Brief'. For each Site Plan document, run the two-phase extraction defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info. Phase 1 extracts all numbered notes and callout annotations (ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION/NOTICE) from every page of the drawing in full. Phase 2 analyses those extracted notes to produce four structured checks: (1) Substation Data — extract the seven Substation Notes fields (Substation Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear with 1.5MVA Siemens RLR override rule, Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing); (2) Assets Field Check Compliance — verify note 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' is present and answered YES; (3) Easement Restriction — check for the swimming pool restriction-on-use-of-land easement note; (4) Easement Substation — check for the padmount substation easement note with dimensions and lot number.
plan_steps.name: Extract Site Plan Information

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only documents where 'document_category' is 'Site Plan'. Must not include any Design Brief documents. Each extraction must include 'filename', 'page_size', 'requires_chunking', 'chunk_strategy', 'estimated_chunks', and 'sub_step_extractions'. The 'sub_step_extractions' object must contain all of the following keys: 'sub-step-extract-all-notes' (complete numbered notes from every page — value must be a non-empty string containing at minimum the first numbered note), 'sub-step-substation-data' (multi-line formatted string with exactly these seven field labels: Substation Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear, Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing — each followed by a colon and a value or NOT FOUND), 'sub-step-field-check' (one of: YES, NOT COMPLETED, NO — NON-COMPLIANT, NOT FOUND — MISSING: this compliance note is mandatory), 'sub-step-easement-restriction' (verbatim note text with lot number, or NOT REQUIRED, or NOT FOUND — REQUIRED), 'sub-step-easement-substation' (verbatim note text with easement dimensions and lot number, or NOT REQUIRED, or NOT FOUND — REQUIRED). The 'page_size' field must be one of: 'A4', 'A3', 'A2', 'A1', 'A0', or 'large-format'. When 'requires_chunking' is true, 'chunk_strategy' must be 'quadrant-split' or 'page-split' and 'estimated_chunks' must be greater than 1. For 'sub-step-substation-data': NOT FOUND values for all fields are acceptable for pole transformer projects that have no padmount substation notes block — do not fail this check solely because all values are NOT FOUND. For 'sub-step-field-check': NOT COMPLETED is a valid result when the note is present but the YES/NO answer has not been filled in by the designer. VARIANCE TOLERANCE: the variance validator applies fuzzy similarity matching (threshold 97%) to long text fields such as 'sub-step-extract-all-notes' — minor punctuation differences, trailing conjunctions (e.g. ', or' vs ','), and equivalent whitespace variations are absorbed and do not count as unique values. Only genuine content differences (e.g. missing or reordered notes, truncated text with similarity below 97%) are flagged as inconsistencies. Structured short fields (substation data values, field-check result, easement text) use exact matching and must be identical across all runs.

plan_steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan") — the same document set as step 5. For each Site Plan document processed in step 5, extract the complete drawing legend (key) from every page using the vision extraction pipeline defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info step 4. Scan every image chunk of each drawing for a LEGEND, KEY, NOTATION, or SYMBOL TABLE panel. Process every visible row in the legend from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections; many legends have 2–4 sections separated by dividers (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements). For each visual element in the legend extract: a plain-English description of the symbol or line style, the exact text label as written on the drawing, a category (one of: cable/equipment/substation/boundary/earthing/annotation/other — REQUIRED on every entry without exception; if uncertain use 'other'), and the page number it was found on. STRICT SCHEMA: each legend entry object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — page, symbol_description, label, category. STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than these four is prohibited — including but not limited to legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or any other key. Category 'earthing' applies to earth conductors and earth electrodes — the label EARTHING maps to category 'earthing', not 'other'. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings have a duct cross-section diagram inside the legend panel with rows like DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — extract every one of these as a regular legend entry (category 'cable' for duct/trench/cable rows, 'earthing' for EARTHING, 'other' for DUCT SPACING NTS annotations). Assemble all entries across all chunks and all pages into a single structured list per document.
plan_steps.name: Extract Drawing Legend

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only Site Plan documents. Each extraction must include 'sub_step_extractions' with the key 'sub-step-extract-legend'. The value of 'sub-step-extract-legend' must be a non-empty JSON array of legend entry objects. Each entry must have EXACTLY four fields: 'page' (integer, 1-based), 'symbol_description' (non-empty string describing the visual symbol or line style — not a label paraphrase), 'label' (non-empty string — the exact verbatim text label from the drawing), and 'category' (string — REQUIRED on every entry, must be one of: cable, equipment, substation, boundary, earthing, annotation, other; NEVER null or absent — use 'other' if uncertain). Category MUST be assigned using pattern rules: 'cable' if label contains TRENCH/CABLE/CONDUCTOR/OVERHEAD MAINS/UNDERGROUND MAINS/DUCT; 'equipment' if label contains POLE/PILLAR/COLUMN/LANTERN/SLCP/SHACKLE/JOINT/UGOH/HV ABS/HV USL/LV LINK or is a short equipment code; 'substation' if label contains SUBSTATION/KIOSK; 'boundary' if label contains EASEMENT/DEMARCATION/BOUNDARY/LGA; 'earthing' if label is EARTHING; otherwise 'other'. Entries with extra fields beyond these four (such as legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, or any other key) indicate schema drift and must be flagged. The array must contain at least one entry. Entries with null or empty symbol_description or label indicate incomplete extraction and must be flagged.

plan_steps.description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=6 (document_category="TAL"). TAL files do not require chunking — run this step directly after Step 2 without needing Steps 3, 4, or 5. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those entries with document_category of exactly "TAL" (Technical Asset List). These are the spreadsheet files (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) identified in Step 1. For each TAL document, use the proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet process as the extraction guide. Each spreadsheet entry from Step 2 includes worksheet_count (total sheets), worksheets_to_analyse (non-empty sheets), and a worksheets array listing each sheet name with its row_count and column_count — use these to plan how many extraction passes are needed. For each non-empty worksheet: identify the header row, map column names to standard asset fields (Asset ID, Serial Number, Asset Type, Description, Location, Address, Feeder, Voltage Level, Capacity Rating, Manufacturer, Model, Condition Rating, Status, Installation Date, Last Inspection Date), and extract every data row as a structured asset record. Include source_sheet and source_document in every record. Produce a consolidated output with all asset_records across all sheets and all TAL files. OUTPUT FILE: The agent saves a dedicated AssetExtract_{timestamp}.json file to the job output directory. This file contains: process_id, timestamp, source_documents (list of spreadsheet filenames), total_assets (integer), and asset_records (flat array of all extracted asset records across all sheets and all TAL files). This file is stored in state as asset_extraction and is available for use by Step 7 (report consolidation) and any subsequent steps.
plan_steps.name: Extract Asset Spreadsheet Data

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with "total_files" integer and "extractions" array. Each extraction entry must include "filename", "total_sections", "relevant_sections", and "step_extraction" containing "asset_records" (array), "total_assets" (integer), and "source_documents" (array). Every asset_record must have at minimum: asset_id or serial_number (at least one non-null), asset_type, and source_sheet. Every asset_record must also include "superior_functional_location" (string or null) — the value from the 'Superior Function', 'Superior FL', 'Functional Location', or 'FLOC' column if present, otherwise null. Only documents with document_category "TAL" must be processed — no PDF or Design Brief files. The response must also include asset_extract_file (path to AssetExtract_*.json) and asset_extract.total_assets (integer count of all extracted asset records).

plan_steps.description: Using the asset records from Step 7, the text annotations extracted from the site plan in Step 5 (sub-step-extract-all-notes), and the drawing legend from Step 6, locate each asset in the site plan drawing text and identify which legend symbol is associated with it. For each asset record: (1) Strip all leading alphabetic prefix characters and any leading zeros from the asset_id to produce a bare numeric ID (e.g. SLGT00302876 → 302876, CB00045 → 45, TX0001234 → 1234). (2) Search the Step 5 extracted site plan text for the bare numeric ID as a text token or label — asset IDs appear as numeric labels adjacent to their drawing symbols in the annotated text. (3) When found, examine the text context immediately surrounding the bare ID to infer which drawing symbol is associated with it, then match that context to the best-fitting legend entry from Step 6 (comparing symbol descriptions and labels). Set symbol_description and label from the matched legend entry and match_status to 'found'. (4) If the bare ID is not found in the extracted text, set symbol_description and label to null and match_status to 'not_found'. Output an updated_assets array containing every original asset record from Step 7 with four new fields added: bare_id, symbol_description, label, and match_status. Also output asset_symbol_matches (the raw per-asset match results) and match_summary totals.
plan_steps.name: Match Assets to Site Plan Drawing Symbols

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'updated_assets' (array), 'asset_symbol_matches' (array), and 'match_summary' (object). 'updated_assets' must contain every original asset record from Step 7 with four additional fields: 'bare_id' (numeric-only string, no prefix letters, no leading zeros), 'symbol_description' (string or null), 'label' (string or null), and 'match_status' (exactly 'found' or 'not_found'). All original TAL fields must be preserved. 'asset_symbol_matches' must contain one entry per asset with 'asset_id', 'bare_id', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'match_status'. 'match_summary' must include 'total', 'found', and 'not_found' integer counts that sum to total. A not_found count equal to total is acceptable when no asset IDs appear in the extracted site plan text — this is not a failure. Entries with match_status 'found' must have non-null symbol_description and label.

plan_steps.description: Using the asset records from Step 7 and the symbol matches from Step 8, produce a fully enriched asset register via two-phase SFL linkage. Phase A — SFL lookup: for each asset whose own bare ID was not found in Step 8 (match_status 'not_found'), read the asset's 'superior_functional_location' field from the Step 7 TAL data (e.g. 'POLE01011307'), strip its prefix and leading zeros to a bare number (e.g. '1011307'), and check whether that bare number is present in Step 8's found_bare_map. If the superior asset WAS found on the drawing, inherit its symbol_description and legend label and set match_confidence to 'high'. Example: SLGT00316949 has superior_functional_location='POLE01011307'; bare SFL number is '1011307'; Step 8 found 1011307 with symbol 'Small hollow open circle' and label 'NEW POLE'; SLGT00316949 is therefore enriched with that label and confidence 'high'. Phase B — SFL chunk search: for assets whose SFL bare number was not in Step 8's found_bare_map (i.e. the superior asset was not itself in the TAL), search the site plan drawing chunks visually for the SFL bare number, and when found identify the adjacent symbol. Assets with no SFL field and those whose SFL was not found anywhere receive match_confidence 'none'.
plan_steps.name: Enrich Assets with Legend Data

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'enriched_assets' (array) and 'match_summary' (object). Every asset record from Step 7 must appear in 'enriched_assets'. Each entry must contain all original TAL fields plus: 'symbol_description' (string or null), 'legend_label' (string or null), 'legend_category' (string or null), 'match_confidence' (one of: high, none), and 'match_method' (one of: direct, sfl_lookup, sfl_chunk_search, sfl_not_found, no_sfl). 'match_summary' must include 'total', 'found_direct', 'found_via_sfl', 'found_on_drawing', and 'not_found' integer counts. Assets with match_method 'direct' or 'sfl_lookup' or 'sfl_chunk_search' must have match_confidence 'high'. A not_found count equal to total is acceptable when Step 8 found no matches and no SFL fields are populated — do not fail solely on this basis.

plan_steps.description: Merge the design brief extraction (Step 4), site plan extraction (Step 5), and enriched asset register (Step 9) into a single unified Customer Connections Application Review report. Summarize all extracted supply requirements, funding items, site plan data, and the enriched asset list (with symbol labels identifying each asset as NEW, EXISTING, or REMOVE) in a consolidated JSON report.
plan_steps.name: Consolidate Application Review Report

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'report_type', 'generated_at', 'source_extractions' (containing keys for design brief, site plan, asset extraction, and enriched asset results), and a 'summary' section with 'total_documents_processed' and 'extraction_keys'.

plan_steps.description: Using all extracted inputs from the Design Brief (Step 4), Site Plan (Steps 5–6), and enriched asset register (Step 9), run a structured four-task Customer Connections Review. Task 1: Consolidate all determination of funding requirements from the Design Brief into a structured funding details section. Task 2: Compare the method of supply requirements from the Design Brief against the Site Plan and list any scope missing from the site plan. Task 3: Compare the funding arrangement outlined in the Design Brief against the Design Drawing and list any scope missing from the drawing. Task 4: Produce a complete asset register listing every asset with its legend information, action status (new/remove/replace/existing), and found-on-diagram status.
plan_steps.name: Customer Connections Review Analysis

validation.criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'analysis_report' containing four keys: 'funding_details' (object with contestable/non-contestable/ancillary cost arrays), 'method_of_supply_comparison' (object with missing_from_site_plan array), 'funding_arrangement_comparison' (object with missing_from_drawing array), and 'asset_register' (array of asset records each with legend_label, action_status, match_confidence, found_on_diagram). 'consolidated_report_path' (string) must be present linking to the Step 10 completion test report. A partial result with some tasks completed is acceptable — do not fail solely on missing tasks if the analytics agent returned data.

step_output.search_context:
Scan the project document folder for all relevant documents. Use the search context and document categorisation rules from the process knowledge files (proc-extract-design-brief-info, proc-extract-site-plan-info, and proc-extract-asset-spreadsheet) to determine which files to include and how to classify each document type. For each file found, read the document content and classify it as one of: 'Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', 'Town Planning Report', 'Reticulation Drawing', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Diagram', 'Network Diagram', or 'TAL'. Any Excel spreadsheet file (.xlsx or .xls) containing asset register, equipment schedule, or inventory data (columns for Asset ID, Asset Type, Location, Condition Rating, or similar) must be classified as 'TAL' (Technical Asset List). Classification must be based on document content — what the document actually contains — not its filename. Produce a structured document list with metadata for all matched files.

=== Knowledge: Extract Information from Design Brief ===
Search Context: Design brief documents for a customer connections application submitted to Endeavour Energy. Target documents include: project design briefs (PDF documents containing a 'Method of Supply Requirement' section and a 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section), customer connection offer documents, and any associated architectural, services, or town planning reports. Key filename indicators: documents with 'DESIGN_BRIEF', 'BRIEF', or 'ARP' (Endeavour Energy project reference) in the filename. Key content indicators: structured sections covering HV Works, LV Works, SL Works, Earthing, Easements, Substation works, and a funding determination table listing items with responsible party and funding category. Exclude: reticulation drawings and engineering drawing files identified by 'RETIC' or 'DWG' in the filename — these are Site Plan documents and must be assigned document_category 'Site Plan'.
Document Category 'Design Brief': Positive filename patterns: ["Contains 'BRIEF' or 'brief' (e.g. DESIGN_BRIEF, Design_Brief)", "Contains 'DB' as a standalone component of the filename", "Contains 'ARP' followed by a project number (typical Endeavour Energy design brief naming convention)", "Contains 'DESIGN' in conjunction with project or ARP references", "Contains 'architectural', 'services', 'structural', or 'town_planning'"]. Document type values: ['Design Brief', 'Architectural Brief', 'Services Brief', 'Structural Brief', 'Town Planning Report', 'Connection Offer', 'Customer Connection Brief']. Negative filename patterns (exclude from this category): ["Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (indicates a reticulation drawing)", "Contains 'DWG' (indicates an engineering drawing file)", "Contains 'SITEPLAN' or 'site_plan'", "Contains 'SUBSTATION' or 'substation' as the primary descriptor (not as a section heading within a brief)"].

=== Knowledge: Extract Information from Site Plans ===
Search Context: Site plan drawings for an electrical network project submitted to Endeavour Energy. Target documents include: electrical network diagrams showing HV and LV infrastructure, substation layout drawings (pole substations, padmount substations, kiosk substations), site layout plans with electrical asset annotations, and engineering drawings containing a 'Substation Notes' or 'Sub Notes' callout table. Key filename indicators: documents with 'RETIC' (reticulation drawing), 'DWG' (engineering drawing), or a drawing number prefix (e.g. D followed by digits, A followed by digits) in the filename. Key content indicators: a Substation Notes annotation block, a NOTES section with numbered notes, ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION callouts, easement creation notes, and field check compliance notes. Engineering drawings produced to Endeavour Energy or similar network operator standards. Target file types: PDF engineering drawings, scanned drawing sets. Exclude: design brief documents identified by 'BRIEF' or 'DESIGN_BRIEF' in the filename.
Document Category 'Site Plan': Positive filename patterns: ["Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (e.g. ARP4983_RETIC, D526458_ARP4983_RETIC — indicates a reticulation drawing)", "Contains 'DWG' as part of the filename (indicates an engineering drawing)", "Contains 'SITEPLAN', 'SITE_PLAN', or 'site_plan'", "Contains 'SUBSTATION' or 'SUB' as the primary descriptor (not as a section heading within a brief)", 'Drawing number prefix format (e.g. D followed by digits, A followed by digits) typical of network operator drawing numbering conventions', "Contains 'ELEC', 'ELECTRICAL', or 'NETWORK' in conjunction with a drawing reference"]. Document type values: ['Reticulation Drawing', 'Electrical Network Diagram', 'Site Plan Drawing', 'Substation Drawing', 'Network Diagram', 'Engineering Drawing', 'HV Network Diagram', 'LV Network Diagram', 'Pole Substation Drawing', 'Padmount Substation Drawing']. Negative filename patterns (exclude from this category): ["Contains 'BRIEF' or 'brief' (e.g. DESIGN_BRIEF, Design_Brief)", "Contains 'ARP' as a standalone prefix without a drawing number suffix"].

=== Knowledge: Extract Information from Asset Spreadsheet ===
Search Context: Asset register or asset management spreadsheet files submitted as part of a project document package. Target documents include: Excel spreadsheets (.xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, .xlsb) containing lists of electrical network assets (transformers, switchgear, cables, poles, substations, meters), asset inventory registers, asset condition assessment spreadsheets, and equipment schedules. Key filename indicators: documents with 'ASSET', 'REGISTER', 'SCHEDULE', 'INVENTORY', 'EQUIPMENT', or 'PLANT' in the filename. Key content indicators: tabular data with columns for asset identifiers, asset types, locations, condition ratings, and technical specifications. Exclude: PDF documents and engineering drawings — these are covered by separate processes.
Document Category 'TAL': Positive filename patterns: ["Contains 'ASSET' or 'asset' (e.g. ASSET_REGISTER, Asset_List)", "Contains 'REGISTER' or 'register'", "Contains 'SCHEDULE' or 'schedule' in the filename", "Contains 'INVENTORY' or 'inventory'", "Contains 'EQUIPMENT' or 'equipment'", "Contains 'PLANT' in conjunction with register or list", 'Has file extension .xlsx, .xls, .xlsm, or .xlsb']. Document type values: ['Asset Register', 'Asset Schedule', 'Equipment Schedule', 'Plant Register', 'Asset Inventory', 'Asset Condition Assessment', 'Network Asset Register']. Negative filename patterns (exclude from this category): ["Contains 'RETIC' or 'retic' (indicates a reticulation drawing)", "Contains 'DWG' (indicates an engineering drawing file)", "Contains 'BRIEF' or 'brief' (indicates a design brief)"].

process_step.details: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=4 (document_category="Design Brief"). TAL spreadsheet files and Site Plan files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those documents that meet the search context of the 'Extract Information from Design Brief' process (proc-extract-design-brief-info). A document qualifies if its content matches the search context criteria: it must contain a 'Method of Supply Requirement' section and/or a 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section, or be an architectural, services, or town planning report. Do not process any document that does not meet this content criteria, even if it was tagged as 'Design Brief' in Step 2 — and do not process any Site Plan or drawing document. For each qualifying document, extract structured information using proc-extract-design-brief-info as the extraction guide. Extract supply requirements (HV, LV, Street Lighting, Earthing, Easements, Substation) and funding determination items.

expected_output.description: Step 1: Identify and Review Design Brief Documents — A DocumentReview JSON file listing all matched documents with metadata and quality indicators. | Step 2: Plan Document Processing Approach — A ProcessingPlan JSON file with a processing entry per matched document. | Step 3: Extract Supply Requirements from Design Brief — Structured supply requirements extracted from the design brief documents. | Step 4: Extract Determination of Funding Requirements — Structured funding determination items extracted from the design brief.

sub_steps.details: Identify and consolidate all High Voltage (HV) information by navigating technical requirements, visual markups, and funding determinations.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Primary HV Method of Supply (MOS)
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the 'Method of Supply Requirement' section. Identify: 1) Removal of Assets (e.g., OH HV cables between specific pole refs). 2) Installation of Assets (e.g., new OH HV cable locations). 3) Voltage Specifics (verify line types as HV or LV, checking for mislabeled requirements in LV sections).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Review Visual HV Markups and Annotations
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Examine the site sketch. Cross-reference text with visual data to identify: 1) Cable Specifications (e.g., 'NEW 240M XEE HV Cable' or 'New OH 7/4.5AMC'). 2) Connection Points (e.g., '2-UGOH Poles'). 3) Substation Context (e.g., 'New Sub' locations).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify HV Infrastructure Funding and Materials
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Determine financial responsibilities. Identify: 1) Reimbursements (e.g., Spare HV duct capital contributions). 2) Customer Charges (e.g., Duct usage charges). 3) Contestability (e.g., Network switching or substation commissioning).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify Compliance Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure HV design meets mandatory codes. Verify requirements for compliance with MDI0031, MCI0005, and NSW Service and Installation Rules.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Consolidate all High Voltage Information
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Group and format all information which relates to High Voltage actions, materials, standards, markups and methods of supply into a single category High Voltage (HV).

sub_steps.details: Based on the provided Design Brief (ARP4983), here are the instructions to locate all information specifically regarding Low Voltage (LV) works for the Elizabeth Street project:

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify the LV Works Section
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Navigate to the section of the document under the heading Method of Supply Requirement. Locate the sub-heading LV Works to find the specific scope of electrical adjustments.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Review Removal and Installation Requirements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Find the instruction to remove the Overhead (OH) LV cable currently located between pole ref '23' and pole ref '20' on the southern side of Elizabeth Drive. Note the requirement to string a new OH cable between pole ref '3' and pole ref '20' on the northern side of Elizabeth Drive. Note: While the brief label for this specific bullet point mentions 'HV cable,' it is categorized strictly under the 'LV Works' section.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Consult the Visual Sketch
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Refer to the diagram to visualize the physical path of the LV asset relocation. Look for the legend or mark-ups indicating 'Remove OH' and 'New OH' to see how the LV lines interact with the northern and southern sides of the road.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify Compliance Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure all LV service works comply with the NSW Service and Installation Rules. Confirm the design adheres to Endeavour Energy standards MDI0031 and MCI0005.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check Service Connections
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the General section regarding the 'New OH road crossing service connection' intended to maintain existing services.

sub_steps.details: Identify and extract all Street Lighting (SL) information by reviewing specialized work sections and visual indicators.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify the SL Works Section
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the 'SL Works' sub-heading under the Method of Supply Requirement section. Identify specific tasks such as 'Remove SL' and 'Install new SL'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Cross-reference with Visual Markups
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Examine the provided sketch for markers or annotations specifically relating to street lighting assets. Verify if SL locations are tied to specific pole references mentioned in HV or LV sections.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check for SL Material Funding
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the funding determination section to see if Street Lighting materials are 'Endeavour Energy Supplied' or if they fall under 'Customer Funded Contestable Works'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify SL Standard Compliance
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Ensure extraction captures any requirement for SL works to comply with specific public lighting standards or council requirements mentioned in the text.

sub_steps.details: Extract all technical requirements regarding grounding and earthing systems for the proposed electrical assets.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Earthing Technical Requirements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search the Design Brief for keywords 'Earthing', 'Grounding', or 'Earth Mat'. Identify requirements for earthing at new substation or switching station locations.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Specific Earthing Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract references to mandatory earthing standards (e.g., MDI or MCI series) that must be met during the detailed design phase.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check Substation Specific Earthing
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the 'Substation & Switching Station Works' section to determine if specific earthing configurations are required for the new pole substation or padmount substation.

sub_steps.details: Identify and document all land interest, easement, and property access requirements specified in the brief.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Land Interest Guidelines
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the 'Land Interests' section in the brief. Verify if the checkbox for 'NA' is selected or if land interests are required for the project.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Required Easements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Extract details regarding required easements for new HV/LV lines or substation sites. Note any reference to the 'Land Interest Guidelines (LIG)'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Check for Property Access Conditions
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Document any specific conditions regarding access to private property or the creation of right-of-ways for Endeavour Energy assets.

sub_steps.details: Capture all specifications and requirements for the removal, installation, or modification of substation assets.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Substation Works Section
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the 'Substation & Switching Station Works' sub-heading. Record specific actions like 'Remove Pole Sub' or 'Install New Pole Sub' at specific references.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Technical Ratings and References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Document any stated ratings (e.g., kVA) or specific equipment references (e.g., 'ref 9' or 'ref 29') for the substation assets.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Substation Commissioning Responsibilities
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Review the funding section to determine if 'substation commissioning' is categorized as a 'Customer Funded Non-Contestable Work'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Verify Substation Clearances and Standards
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Capture requirements for physical clearances or compliance with substation design standards like MDI0031 mentioned in the compliance section.

sub_steps.details: Prompt the LLM to identify and extract all items listed under the 'Determination of Funding Requirements' section or equivalent heading. For each item record: item_description (verbatim or paraphrased), funding_category (e.g. capital contribution, non-contestable, contestable), responsible_party (e.g. applicant, network operator), amount_or_formula (monetary amount or formula if stated), source_section, and source_page.

Prompt the LLM to identify and extract all line items listed under 'Ancillary Network Services Fees', 'Payment Advice', or 'Pro Forma Tax Invoice' sections. For each fee record: item_description (e.g. Standard Connection Offer Fee, Design Information Fee), date, amount (dollar value), and source_section and source_page. Also capture the total amount payable if stated.
Identify and consolidate all logistics required for the applicant to remit payment to Endeavour Energy. [cite: 93]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Preferred Payment Method
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the preferred payment channel, specifically identifying EFT as the primary method. [cite: 94]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Banking Details
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Capture the specific financial coordinates provided, including the BSB (012-003) and Account Number (8376 89858). [cite: 94]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Identify Mandatory Transaction References
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Locate the unique alphanumeric reference code (e.g., ARP4983A2) that must be quoted in the EFT transaction field. [cite: 95]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Document Remittance and Restriction Requirements
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Identify the email for remittance copies (remittances@endeavourenergy.com.au) and note that credit cards and cheques are not accepted. [cite: 95, 96]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Capture Invoice Policy
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Note the condition for receiving a formal Tax Invoice following the receipt of payment. [cite: 97]

Install a 200kVA, 3 Phase Semi Rural Substation on the new 12.5m/12kN Pole 1, with Separate Earthing.

Install a 200kVA, 3 Phase Semi Rural Substation on the new 12.5m/12kN Pole 1, with Separate Earthing.
Remove HV mains between Pole 1 and Pole 2.

Spare HV duct as per Fact Sheet 11
Reimbursements to be paid to Endeavour Energy by Customer: Duct usage charge as required

items.item_description: Spare HV duct as per Fact Sheet 11
Network switching, substation commissioning, contract inspection

Substation Specific Earthing.description: Install a 200kVA, 3 Phase Semi Rural Substation on the new 12.5m/12kN Pole 1, with Separate Earthing.

Confirm the design adheres to Endeavour Energy standards MDI0031 and MCI0005.

   Running independent validation...
   [PASS] Validation: The extraction process correctly handled the Site Plan document, extracting all required fields and adhering to the specified format and criteria. (100%)
   Files validated: input=None, output=ExtractionReport_proc_extract_site_plan_info_20260523_120707_193968.json

process_step.details: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan"). TAL spreadsheet files and Design Brief files must be excluded. Using the processing plan from Step 2, filter the document list to only those where 'document_category' is 'Site Plan'. Do not process any document tagged as 'Design Brief'. For each Site Plan document, run the two-phase extraction defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info. Phase 1 extracts all numbered notes and callout annotations (ATTENTION/WARNING/CAUTION/NOTICE) from every page of the drawing in full. Phase 2 analyses those extracted notes to produce four structured checks: (1) Substation Data — extract the seven Substation Notes fields (Substation Asset Number, Transformer Size, HV Switchgear with 1.5MVA Siemens RLR override rule, Voltage Level, LV Switchgear, Cubicle Size, Earthing); (2) Assets Field Check Compliance — verify note 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' is present and answered YES; (3) Easement Restriction — check for the swimming pool restriction-on-use-of-land easement note; (4) Easement Substation — check for the padmount substation easement note with dimensions and lot number.

expected_output.description: Step 3: Extract Site Plan Information — Five structured extraction outputs per document: complete drawing notes, substation structured data, field check compliance result, and two easement check results.

fields.sub-step-extract-all-notes: Complete list of all numbered notes and standalone callouts from every page, in sequence order
fields.sub-step-substation-data: Formatted string with all seven Substation Notes fields, with 1.5MVA override notation where applicable
fields.sub-step-field-check: 'YES', 'NO', or 'NOT FOUND' result for the assets field-check compliance note
fields.sub-step-easement-restriction: Extracted restriction-on-use-of-land note text with lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' / 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED'
fields.sub-step-easement-substation: Extracted padmount substation easement note text with dimensions and lot number, or 'NOT REQUIRED' / 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED'

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Extract All Drawing Notes and Annotations
sub_steps.details: Extract every textual note and annotation from every page of the drawing in full. This is a comprehensive raw extraction pass — do not filter or summarise. Capture: all numbered notes (1., 2., 3., ...) from the NOTES section with their complete text; all standalone ATTENTION:, WARNING:, CAUTION:, and NOTICE: callout blocks with their complete text; any inline hand-written or red-line markup annotations. Notes that span multiple drawing regions must be assembled into a single complete entry. This output is the source material for all Phase 2 sub-steps.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate and Extract Numbered Notes Block
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan every page of the drawing for a dedicated NOTES section or numbered annotation block. Extract ALL individual notes listed, in sequence order (1., 2., 3., ...). Each note must be captured in full — do not truncate. If a note is split across adjacent image regions (common in large-format drawings), assemble the complete text. Include every note number in the final output — missing numbers indicate an incomplete extraction.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Standalone Callout Annotations
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: In addition to the numbered notes block, scan every page for standalone callout boxes or annotations with labels ATTENTION:, WARNING:, CAUTION:, or NOTICE:. These may appear anywhere on the drawing (not just in the NOTES block) and often contain critical safety, service, or access requirements. Extract each callout's label and its complete text. If the callout appears physically separate from the NOTES block, include it as an additional entry appended after the numbered notes (e.g. 'CAUTION: HIGH PRESSURE GAS MAINS IN VICINITY...').

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Red-line and Hand-written Markups
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Look for hand-written or red-line markup annotations that modify or supplement the printed notes — for example, specific cable types, removal or addition instructions, or project-specific notes. Capture these verbatim and identify them as markups (e.g. '[MARKUP] Remove span between...').

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, locate the Substation Notes annotation block and extract the seven structured data fields. Apply the conditional HV Switchgear rule: if Transformer Size is 1.5MVA, HV Switchgear must be recorded as 'Siemens RLR' regardless of what is written in the drawing, and the override must be explicitly noted in the output. Return all seven fields as a formatted multi-line string.
sub_steps.output_format:
Multi-line formatted string:
Substation Asset Number: [value or NOT FOUND]
Transformer Size: [value or NOT FOUND]
HV Switchgear: [value — if 1.5MVA rule applied, append: OVERRIDDEN per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required]
Voltage Level: [value or NOT FOUND]
LV Switchgear: [value or NOT FOUND]
Cubicle Size: [value or NOT FOUND]
Earthing: [value or NOT FOUND]

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate Substation Notes Block
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search the extracted notes and the drawing for annotations labelled 'Substation Notes', 'Sub Notes', or a similar heading. These are typically found in a table or callout box near the substation symbol or in the drawing title block area. Extract the heading and all fields within it.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract the Seven Data Fields
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: From the Substation Notes block, extract each of the following fields as labelled: Substation Asset Number (unique ID assigned by the network operator, e.g. S1234 or a 6-digit number), Transformer Size (rated capacity with units, e.g. 1.5MVA, 500kVA, 200kVA), HV Switchgear (make and model, e.g. Siemens RLR, ABB SafeRing, or as written), Voltage Level (in kV, e.g. 11kV, 22kV, 33kV), LV Switchgear (make, model, and rating), Cubicle Size (dimensions or type code), and Earthing (type and compliance standard).

instructional_sub_steps.title: Apply 1.5MVA Conditional HV Switchgear Rule
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Check the extracted Transformer Size. If it equals 1.5MVA (or equivalently 1500kVA), then regardless of what the drawing shows for HV Switchgear, record HV Switchgear as 'Siemens RLR' and append the note 'OVERRIDDEN per 1.5MVA rule — Siemens RLR required.' to that field. If the transformer size is not 1.5MVA, record HV Switchgear exactly as written in the Substation Notes.

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, verify that the drawing includes the mandatory assets field-check compliance note. The exact note required is: 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' answered with 'YES'. Return one of three values: 'YES' (note present, answered YES), 'NO' (note present, answered NO — flag as non-compliant), or 'NOT FOUND' (note is absent from the drawing — flag for follow-up).
sub_steps.required_note_text: HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Field Check Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for the text 'HAVE ALL EXISTING ASSETS BEEN FIELD CHECKED AND ARE ACCURATE AT THE TIME OF DRAWING' or a close paraphrase. This note is typically numbered within the NOTES section and followed by 'YES' or 'NO'. Note: in the drawing the word 'DRAWING' may appear as 'DESIGN' — treat both as equivalent.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Read and Return the Answer
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Once the note is located, read the answer that follows it ('YES' or 'NO'). Return 'YES' if the answer is YES. Return 'NO' if the answer is NO (and add a flag: 'NON-COMPLIANT — field check not confirmed'). If the note cannot be found anywhere in the drawing, return 'NOT FOUND' (and add a flag: 'MISSING — this compliance note is mandatory').

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Easement — Restriction on Use of Land (Swimming Pool Setback)
sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, check whether the drawing includes the required restriction-on-use-of-land note relating to swimming pools within the substation easement setback. This note is required for any project that creates a substation easement. Extract the full note text including the lot number(s) referenced. If the note is absent, determine whether this is a padmount substation project — if so, flag as MISSING; if not, flag as NOT REQUIRED.
sub_steps.required_note_template: A RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND, IN RELATION TO SWIMMING POOLS, MEASURED 5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxx]
sub_steps.output_format: Full extracted note text with lot number(s), or 'NOT REQUIRED' (not a substation easement project), or 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED' (substation easement project but note is missing)

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Restriction Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for text containing 'RESTRICTION ON THE USE OF LAND', 'SWIMMING POOL', '5m FROM THE SUBSTATION EASEMENT', or 'IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT'. This note may appear within the numbered NOTES section or as a separate annotation.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Note Text and Lot Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If found, extract the full note text verbatim. Identify and record the lot number(s) referenced after 'WITHIN PROPOSED LOT' — this is a critical compliance detail. Return the complete extracted text as the field value.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Determine Applicability if Not Found
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the note is not found, check whether the project involves a padmount substation (look for 'padmount', 'PADMOUNT', or 'PAD MOUNT' in the Substation Notes or drawing annotations). If a padmount substation is involved, this note is mandatory — return 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED (padmount substation project detected)'. If no padmount substation is present, return 'NOT REQUIRED'.

sub_steps.details: Using the notes extracted in Phase 1, check whether the drawing includes the required padmount substation easement creation note. This note is required for all projects involving a padmount substation. Extract the full note text including the easement dimensions and lot number(s) referenced. If the note is absent and the project involves a padmount substation, flag as MISSING.
sub_steps.required_note_template: AN EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM [x]m x [x]m IS TO BE CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT [xxxx]
sub_steps.output_format: Full extracted note text with easement dimensions and lot number(s), or 'NOT REQUIRED' (not a padmount substation project), or 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED' (padmount substation project but note is missing)

instructional_sub_steps.title: Search for Padmount Easement Note
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Search all extracted notes for text containing 'EASEMENT FOR PADMOUNT SUBSTATION', 'PADMOUNT SUBSTATION MINIMUM', or 'CREATED IN FAVOUR OF ENDEAVOUR ENERGY WITHIN PROPOSED LOT' alongside a dimension or measurement. This note may appear within the numbered NOTES section or as a standalone annotation.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Extract Easement Dimensions and Lot Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If found, extract the full note text verbatim. Specifically identify: (a) the easement dimensions stated after 'MINIMUM' (e.g. '3m x 4m', '4.5m x 6m') and (b) the lot number(s) referenced after 'WITHIN PROPOSED LOT'. Both details are critical — record them clearly in the output.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Determine Applicability if Not Found
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: If the note is not found, check whether the project involves a padmount substation (look for 'padmount', 'PADMOUNT', or 'PAD MOUNT' in the Substation Notes or drawing annotations). If a padmount substation is involved, this note is mandatory — return 'NOT FOUND — REQUIRED (padmount substation project detected)'. If no padmount substation is present, return 'NOT REQUIRED'.

process_step.details: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan") — the same document set as step 5. For each Site Plan document processed in step 5, extract the complete drawing legend (key) from every page using the vision extraction pipeline defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info step 4. Scan every image chunk of each drawing for a LEGEND, KEY, NOTATION, or SYMBOL TABLE panel. Process every visible row in the legend from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections; many legends have 2–4 sections separated by dividers (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements). For each visual element in the legend extract: a plain-English description of the symbol or line style, the exact text label as written on the drawing, a category (one of: cable/equipment/substation/boundary/earthing/annotation/other — REQUIRED on every entry without exception; if uncertain use 'other'), and the page number it was found on. STRICT SCHEMA: each legend entry object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — page, symbol_description, label, category. STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than these four is prohibited — including but not limited to legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or any other key. Category 'earthing' applies to earth conductors and earth electrodes — the label EARTHING maps to category 'earthing', not 'other'. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings have a duct cross-section diagram inside the legend panel with rows like DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — extract every one of these as a regular legend entry (category 'cable' for duct/trench/cable rows, 'earthing' for EARTHING, 'other' for DUCT SPACING NTS annotations). Assemble all entries across all chunks and all pages into a single structured list per document.

expected_output.description: Step 4: Extract Drawing Legend — A structured legend extraction per document: a JSON array of legend entries, each with symbol description, label, category, and page number.

fields.sub-step-extract-legend: JSON array of legend entry objects. Each object must contain EXACTLY these four fields: 'page' (integer), 'symbol_description' (string — visual geometry, not a label paraphrase), 'label' (string — verbatim text from the drawing), 'category' (string — one of: cable, equipment, substation, boundary, earthing, annotation, other — REQUIRED on every entry, never omitted). No additional fields permitted.

sub_steps.sub_step_name: Extract Legend Entries from All Pages
sub_steps.details: Scan every image chunk of the drawing for a legend, key, notation, or symbol table. Process every visible row in the legend panel from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections. Many legends have 2–4 distinct sections (e.g. cables, then equipment, then boundaries, then duct arrangements) separated by dividers or headings; continue until you have reached the last row. For each row, extract: (1) the VISUAL GEOMETRY of the drawn symbol — describe its exact shape, fill, line weight, line pattern, and any interior marks as you see them drawn; (2) the exact text label; (3) the page number; (4) the category — REQUIRED for every entry, no exceptions. CRITICAL RULE: symbol_description must describe what the symbol LOOKS LIKE as a drawn graphic, NOT a paraphrase or echo of the label text. Each legend row has its own unique symbol — do not assign the same visual description to multiple rows. If you see the same description repeating for consecutive rows, you have misread the legend — look again at each row individually. WRONG examples (label paraphrases — never produce these): 'New lantern', 'Existing pole', 'New pillar', 'Remove conductor', 'Underground cable'. CORRECT examples (visual geometry): 'Small hollow circle with short lines radiating outward from the edge', 'Small solid black filled circle', 'Hollow rectangle outline with no fill', 'Solid thin line with evenly spaced short perpendicular tick marks'. REFERENCE TABLE — use these known symbol descriptions when you recognise the visual pattern next to the label: NEW LV TRENCH → Long dashed line; STRING NEW OH CABLE → Short dashed line; EXISTING UNDERGROUND MAINS → Alternating long-short dashed line; EXISTING OH CABLE → Solid thin line; REMOVE CONDUCTOR → Dotted line; EXISTING DUCTS → Thick heavy solid black line; NEW HV TRENCH → Solid line with two diagonal slash marks; EXISTING LANTERN → Small circle with internal cross (four-quadrant cross inside circle); REMOVE LANTERN → Circle with internal cross and starburst outer spikes radiating from edge; NEW LANTERN → Circle with starburst outer spikes and hollow centre; EXISTING POLE → Small solid black filled circle; REMOVE POLE → Small solid black filled circle with large X through it; REPLACE POLE → Circle split vertically half-black half-white; NEW POLE → Small hollow open circle; EXISTING COLUMN → Small solid black filled square; NEW COLUMN → Small hollow open square; EXISTING PILLAR → Solid black filled rectangle; NEW PILLAR → Hollow rectangle outline; PADMOUNT SUBSTATION → Rectangle containing two triangles touching at their points (bowtie shape); POLE SUBSTATION → Circle with triangle inside; LV LINK (N/O) → Small circle with short vertical ticks on top and bottom; HV ABS (N/C) → Circle with horizontal line through the centre; HV USL (N/C) → Semi-circle dome with horizontal base line; HIGH PRESSURE GAS → Solid line with GAS text centred along it; WATER MAIN → Solid line with W characters spaced evenly along it; LGA DEMARCATION → Thin solid line; NEW FREEWAY BOUNDARY → Faint light grey long dashed line; ENDEAVOUR ENERGY EASEMENT → Faint light grey medium dashed line. When you see a symbol that matches one of these, use the reference description exactly.
sub_steps.output_format:
JSON array of legend entry objects. STRICT SCHEMA: Each object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — no more, no fewer: 'page' (integer, 1-based), 'symbol_description' (string — visual geometry only), 'label' (string — verbatim label text), 'category' (string — REQUIRED, see values below). STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than 'page', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'category' is strictly prohibited — this includes but is not limited to: legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or ANY other key you might think of. If you find yourself wanting to add another field, do NOT — instead map the information into symbol_description or label. Extra fields cause downstream comparison failures and will invalidate the entire extraction. CATEGORY RULES — 'category' is MANDATORY for every entry without exception — NEVER null. Use these PATTERN RULES in priority order: (1) 'cable' — label contains TRENCH, CABLE, CONDUCTOR, OVERHEAD MAINS, UNDERGROUND MAINS, OVERHEAD LINE, DUCT, or SPARE DUCT (examples: NEW LV TRENCH, EXISTING OVERHEAD MAINS, NEW UNDERGROUND MAINS, EXISTING UNDERGROUND CABLE, EXISTING OH CABLE, NEW OVERHEAD CONDUCTOR, REMOVE CONDUCTOR, REMOVE EXISTING OVERHEAD, STRING NEW OH CABLE, SPARE DUCT, DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, NEW TRENCH); (2) 'equipment' — label contains POLE, PILLAR, COLUMN, LANTERN, SLCP, SL FLOOD, SL LANTERN, SL NIGHT WATCH, SHACKLE, JOINT, UGOH, HV ABS, HV USL, LV LINK, SWITCH, or FUSE, OR is a short equipment code (examples: EXISTING POLE, NEW POLE, REMOVE POLE, REPLACE POLE, EXISTING COLUMN, NEW COLUMN, EXISTING PILLAR, NEW PILLAR, EXISTING LANTERN, NEW LANTERN, REMOVE LANTERN, NEW SL FLOOD, NEW SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL NIGHT WATCH, NEW SLCP ZELL, EXISTING SLCP ZELL, REMOVE SL LANTERN, HV ABS (N/C), HV USL (N/C), EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE, NEW HV ABS CLOSE, EXISTING HV USL CLOSED, LV LINK (N/O), JOINT, END SHACKLE, UGOH, IE 7.0 IA, IEB5, TYPE 2 RAGBOLT); (3) 'substation' — label contains SUBSTATION or KIOSK; (4) 'boundary' — label contains EASEMENT, DEMARCATION, BOUNDARY, or LGA; (5) 'earthing' — label is EARTHING or contains EARTH; (6) 'annotation' — dimension or callout; (7) 'other' — anything not matched above (e.g. TYPE 04 DUCT ARRANGEMENT, FOOTPATH, RMS ROAD, COMPACTED BACKFILL, POLYMERIC CABLE COVER, ROAD LEVEL, DUCT SPACING NTS, XXXXX). If genuinely uncertain, ALWAYS use 'other' — NEVER leave category null or absent. Each row in the legend must produce a UNIQUE symbol_description — if two consecutive entries have identical symbol_descriptions you have made an error and must re-examine each row. Example output:
[
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Long dashed line", "label": "NEW LV TRENCH", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Solid thin line", "label": "EXISTING OH CABLE", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Dotted line", "label": "REMOVE CONDUCTOR", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Thick heavy solid black line", "label": "EXISTING DUCTS", "category": "cable" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small hollow open circle", "label": "NEW POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small solid black filled circle", "label": "EXISTING POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Small solid black filled circle with large X through it", "label": "REMOVE POLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Asterisk", "label": "EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Filled circle", "label": "END SHACKLE", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Circle with starburst outer spikes and hollow centre", "label": "NEW LANTERN", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Hollow rectangle outline", "label": "NEW PILLAR", "category": "equipment" },
  { "page": 1, "symbol_description": "Rectangle containing two triangles touching at their points", "label": "PADMOUNT SUBSTATION", "category": "substation" },

instructional_sub_steps.title: Locate the Legend Area
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Scan the drawing for a box, panel, or area labelled 'LEGEND', 'KEY', 'NOTATION', 'SYMBOL KEY', or 'DRAWING LEGEND'. This is typically found in the bottom-right or bottom-left corner of the drawing, within or adjacent to the title block. On multi-page drawings, the legend may appear only on page 1 — if so, note it as page 1. If no dedicated legend box is found, check for an unlabelled key or symbol table embedded in the drawing.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Describe Each Symbol Individually — Each Row Has Its Own Unique Symbol
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: CRITICAL: Every row in the legend has its own distinct drawn symbol. Each symbol_description must be unique — if you find yourself writing the same description for two or more consecutive rows, you have misread the legend and must look again. For LINE symbols: describe the line pattern precisely — solid/dashed/dotted/chain-dashed, thin/thick/heavy, and any decorations spaced along it (tick marks, letters like W or GAS, diagonal slashes, arrows). For POINT/ICON symbols: describe the outer shape (circle/square/rectangle/triangle), fill (hollow/solid black/half-filled), and any interior marks (cross, X, diagonal line, triangle, bowtie). Use the REFERENCE TABLE in the details field when you recognise a known symbol — match it and use the reference description. Ask yourself before writing each entry: 'Is this description unique and geometric? Could a reader draw exactly this symbol from my words alone without reading the label?'

instructional_sub_steps.title: Copy the Label Verbatim
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: For the 'label' field, copy the exact text written next to the symbol on the drawing, preserving capitalisation and spacing. Do not paraphrase or abbreviate. If a symbol has no label, record label as '[UNLABELLED]'. If a label has no visible symbol, record symbol_description as '[NO SYMBOL]'.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Assign Category — MANDATORY for Every Entry
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: MANDATORY: 'category' is a required field for EVERY entry — NEVER null, NEVER omitted. If genuinely uncertain, use 'other'. PATTERN RULES — apply these in order: (1) 'cable': label contains any of TRENCH, CABLE, CONDUCTOR, OVERHEAD MAINS, UNDERGROUND MAINS, OVERHEAD LINE, DUCT, SPARE DUCT — examples: NEW LV TRENCH, EXISTING OVERHEAD MAINS, NEW UNDERGROUND MAINS, EXISTING UNDERGROUND CABLE, NEW OVERHEAD CONDUCTOR, REMOVE CONDUCTOR, REMOVE EXISTING OVERHEAD, STRING NEW OH CABLE. (2) 'equipment': label contains any of POLE, PILLAR, COLUMN, LANTERN, STREETLIGHT, SLCP, SL FLOOD, SL LANTERN, SL NIGHT WATCH, SHACKLE, JOINT, UGOH, HV ABS, HV USL, LV LINK, SWITCH, FUSE, METER, or label is a short equipment code (UGOH, IE 7.0 IA, IEB5, XXXXX treated as placeholder equipment code) — examples: EXISTING POLE, NEW POLE, REMOVE POLE, REPLACE POLE, EXISTING COLUMN, NEW COLUMN, EXISTING PILLAR, NEW PILLAR, EXISTING LANTERN, NEW LANTERN, REMOVE LANTERN, NEW SL FLOOD, EXISTING SL LANTERN, EXISTING SL NIGHT WATCH, NEW SLCP ZELL, EXISTING SLCP ZELL, REMOVE SL LANTERN, HV ABS (N/C), HV USL (N/C), EXISTING HV ABS CLOSE - REMOVE, NEW HV ABS CLOSE, EXISTING HV USL CLOSED, LV LINK (N/O), JOINT, END SHACKLE, UGOH. (3) 'substation': label contains SUBSTATION or KIOSK. (4) 'boundary': label contains EASEMENT, DEMARCATION, BOUNDARY, or LGA. (5) 'earthing': label is EARTHING or contains EARTH CONDUCTOR or EARTH ELECTRODE. (6) 'annotation': label is a dimension, callout, or arrow descriptor. (7) 'other': anything not matching above — includes TYPE 04 DUCT ARRANGEMENT, FOOTPATH, RMS ROAD, COMPACTED BACKFILL, POLYMERIC CABLE COVER, ROAD LEVEL, DUCT SPACING NTS, XXXXX (if not treating as equipment code). Apply pattern 1 first, then 2, then 3, then 4, then 5, then fall back to 'other'. NEVER leave category null.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Tag to Page Number
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: Tag each entry with the page number from the chunk metadata. If the same legend appears on multiple pages, record it once tagged to page 1. If different pages have different or updated legends, record separate entries per page.

instructional_sub_steps.title: Exhaustiveness Check — Do Not Stop Early
instructional_sub_steps.instructions: After completing your initial extraction pass, count the total number of entries extracted. Then re-scan the legend panel to verify you have not stopped at a section boundary. Many legends are divided into multiple sections (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements, miscellaneous) separated by horizontal rules or section headings — ensure every section has been processed. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings include a separate duct arrangement cross-section diagram area at the bottom of the legend panel, often showing a schematic cross-section of underground ducts. This area contains its own rows with labels such as DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — these are regular legend entries and MUST be extracted like any other row. Assign them category 'cable' for duct/cable/trench entries, 'earthing' for EARTHING, and 'other' for annotations like DUCT SPACING NTS. If you see a cross-section diagram in the legend area, always extract every labelled row from it. If the legend contains 10 or more visible rows but you have extracted fewer than 5 entries, you have missed most of the legend and must re-scan. Also verify: (1) every entry has the 'category' field present and is never null; (2) no entry has extra fields beyond the four required (page, symbol_description, label, category) — any other field name is a violation; (3) the 'label' field contains the exact verbatim text from the drawing, not a paraphrase. If any entry fails these checks, correct it before returning the result.

test_cases.description: Verify that the 'sub-step-extract-legend' key is present in the output for each document.
test_cases.expected_output: The 'sub-step-extract-legend' key should be present.
test_cases.execution_notes: Checked the presence of 'sub-step-extract-legend' key in the output data.
test_cases.reasoning: The key was found as expected, indicating the extraction step was executed.

test_cases.test_name: Verify schema compliance for legend entries
test_cases.description: Ensure each legend entry contains exactly four fields: 'page', 'symbol_description', 'label', and 'category'.
test_cases.expected_output: Each entry should have exactly four fields.
test_cases.actual_output: All entries have exactly four fields.
test_cases.execution_notes: Reviewed each legend entry to confirm the presence of only the four required fields.
test_cases.reasoning: The entries adhered to the strict schema requirements, with no extra fields present.

test_cases.description: Ensure 'symbol_description' and 'label' fields are non-empty for each legend entry.
test_cases.expected_output: All 'symbol_description' and 'label' fields should be non-empty.
test_cases.execution_notes: Verified that each entry's 'symbol_description' and 'label' fields contained text.
test_cases.reasoning: The fields were populated as required, indicating complete extraction.

test_cases.description: Check that categories are assigned correctly based on label content.
test_cases.input_data: Labels from legend entries: 'EARTHING', '125mm Duct', 'Pole Substation - New'.
test_cases.expected_output: Categories should be 'earthing', 'cable', and 'substation' respectively.
test_cases.execution_notes: Cross-referenced labels with category rules. Found mismatches in category assignments.
test_cases.reasoning: The category for 'EARTHING' was incorrectly assigned as 'other', and 'Pole Substation - New' as 'equipment'.

test_cases.description: Ensure all legend entries from the source are present in the output.
test_cases.input_data: Source text mentions 'Remove Pole 3', 'Install LV UGOH'.
test_cases.expected_output: Entries for 'Remove Pole 3' and 'Install LV UGOH' should be present.
test_cases.actual_output: Entries for 'Remove Pole 3' and 'Install LV UGOH' are missing.
test_cases.execution_notes: Compared source text with extracted entries. Noted missing entries.
test_cases.reasoning: The absence of these entries indicates incomplete extraction.

test_cases.description: Ensure each entry is tagged with the correct page number.
test_cases.expected_output: All entries should be tagged with page number 1.
test_cases.actual_output: All entries are tagged with page number 1.
test_cases.execution_notes: Checked the page number field for each entry.
test_cases.reasoning: All entries were correctly tagged, matching the single-page document.

test_cases.description: Ensure there are no duplicate entries in the legend extraction.
test_cases.expected_output: No duplicate entries should be present.
test_cases.execution_notes: Reviewed entries for duplicates by comparing 'label' and 'symbol_description'.
test_cases.reasoning: No duplicates were detected, indicating consistent extraction.

test_cases.test_name: Verify extraction of duct arrangement section
test_cases.description: Ensure all entries from the duct arrangement section are extracted.
test_cases.input_data: Source text includes '125mm Duct - Spare', '125mm Duct - with New Cable'.
test_cases.expected_output: Entries for '125mm Duct - Spare' and '125mm Duct - with New Cable' should be present.
test_cases.actual_output: Entries for '125mm Duct - Spare' and '125mm Duct - with New Cable' are present.
test_cases.execution_notes: Cross-referenced source text with extracted entries for duct arrangement.
test_cases.reasoning: All relevant entries were extracted, indicating completeness in this section.

test_cases.description: Ensure no extra fields are present in the legend entries.
test_cases.expected_output: No extra fields beyond 'page', 'symbol_description', 'label', 'category'.
test_cases.execution_notes: Reviewed each entry for adherence to the strict schema.
test_cases.reasoning: The entries conformed to the schema, with no additional fields present.

test_cases.description: Ensure all high-voltage assets are extracted from the legend.
